content_html:
MKT10007 Lecture 8
Dr Agung Yoga Sembada (Yoga)
Dr Gordon Campbell

Lecture 8
The 3rd P: Promotion
Five promotion mix tools for communicating value to our customer
The changing communications landscape and the need for integrated marketing communications
The communication process and the steps in developing effective marketing communications

“P” for Promotion - communicating value to our customers

The Promotion Mix
Promotion is communication by marketers that informs, persuades and/or reminds consumers of a product in order to influence an opinion or elicit a response
The promotion mix is the  specific blend of promotion tools that the company uses to persuasively communicate customer value and build customer relationships

Role of promotion in the marketing mix
LO1

Communication
The process by which we exchange or share meanings through a common set of symbols:
Interpersonal communication: direct, face-to-face communication between two or more people
Mass communication: communication to large audiences
LO1

Promotion Mix
Advertising: Impersonal, one-way mass communication about a product or organisation that is paid for by a marketer
Direct marketing: Techniques used to get consumers to make a purchase from their home, office or other non-retail setting
Personal selling: Planned presentation to one or more prospective buyers for the purpose of making a sale
Sales promotion: Other marketing activities that stimulate consumer buying and dealer effectiveness
Public relations: Evaluates public attitudes, identifies areas within the organisation that the public may be interested in and executes a program of action to earn public understanding and acceptance

Promotion Mix

Advertising
Advertising is any form of non-personal, paid communication in which the product or company is identified
Environment is increasingly hostile and cluttered which makes it difficult to hold the attention and involvement of consumers
“Half the money I spend on advertising is wasted; the trouble is I don't know which half.”
John Wanamaker (1838-1922)

Types of Advertising
Institutional advertising
Designed to enhance a company’s image rather than promote a particular product
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6c9FEQQt6cc My Mom looks like GE
A particular form of institutional advertising is advocacy advertising, where an organisation expresses its view on controversial issues or responds to media attacks
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AH5R56jILag Dominos

Types of Advertising
Product advertising
Promotes the benefits of a specific good or service
Three main categories:
Pioneer advertising: Designed to stimulate primary demand for a new product or product category
Competitive advertising: Designed to influence demand for a specific brand
Comparative advertising: Compares two or more specifically named or shown competing brands on one or more specific attributes
https://youtu.be/iD9XNUYHCNQ?t=10 Benz

Personal selling
Personal selling is interpersonal communication between a sales representative and one or more prospective buyers for the purpose of making sales and building customer relationships
Offers several advantages over other forms of promotion:
Provides a detailed explanation or demonstration of product
Message can be varied to fit the needs of each prospective customer
Can be directed to specific qualified prospects

Sales vs Marketing
Salespeople are an effective link between the company and its customers to produce customer value and company profit by
Representing the company to customers
Representing customers to the company
Working closely with marketing

“To be a marketing manager without ever doing sales is like a general who never fired a gun”

Direct & Digital marketing
Direct marketing refers to techniques used to get consumers to make a purchase from their home, office or other non-retail setting
Techniques used include personal retailing, direct mail, catalogues and mail order, telemarketing and electronic retailing
Offers customers increased convenience and flexibility, and at the same time can allow marketers to directly target the particular needs of specific customers
https://www.facebook.com/business/ads
Read this “How To” from Facebook about how to create ads>>

Sales promotion
Sales promotion consists of short-term incentives to motivate consumers or members of the distribution channel to purchase a good or service immediately, either by lowering the price or by adding value
Sales promotion =/= discounts

Public relations
Public relations involves evaluating public attitudes, identifying issues that may elicit public concern and executing programs to gain public understanding and acceptance
May involve programs that capitalise on the factors that enhance the firm's image and minimise factors that could generate a negative image
Often associated with publicity
E.g. articles or editorials in publications, human-interest stories on radio or television programs or donating products to worthy causes
Usually initiated through a press release

Functions of public relations
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yrxRCTUt6OY Thank you for smoking

Part 2. The Need for Integrated Marketing Communications
Multi-touch point strategy of communication
e.g:
https://vimeo.com/130894027 Coke ad - Great example of Integration

The Need for Integrated Marketing Communications
Integrated marketing  communications (IMC) involves carefully integrating and coordinating the company’s many communications channels to deliver a clear, consistent, and compelling message about the organization and its products
Consumers don’t think in terms of the different elements of promotion
Marketers often neglect this when planning promotional messages

What firms think about consumers’ reactions to campaigns: (as if!)

Factors affecting the promotional mix
Nature of the product
Product Life Cycle
Cost and risk
Type of buying decision
Routine vs complex
Target market characteristics
Degree of knowledge about brand
Geographic dispersion or concentration
Available funds for promotion

https://vimeo.com/61533199 Susan Glenn - reinvigorating a fading brand
Product Life Cycle

Product Life Cycle
Introduction
Advertising and PR to all inform and aware (first product class, then brand)
Sales promotion and direct marketing to induce trial
Personal selling to gain distribution coverage – get retailers to stock
Growth
Promotion blend shifts
Advertising and PR still used to emphasise differential advantage and build loyalty (e.g “I’m a Mac”)
Sales promotion reduced
https://www.thebalancecareers.com/what-is-a-whale-2917364
https://www.gamasutra.com/view/feature/195806/chasing_the_whale_examining_the_.php
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=--1_WPZVyro
Read these re sales strategies for Introduction and Growth

Product Life Cycle
Maturity
Fierce competition requires persuasive and reminder advertising
Sales promotion re-introduced.
Decline
Advertising greatly reduced
Sales promotion and personal selling maintained at low levels

Product Life Cycle
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1RAkZUt2rA4 intel inside – Pull ad
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EKcHiSjiMMw aus made – Pull ad

Part 3. The steps in developing effective marketing communications
Integrated marketing  communications (IMC) = delivering a clear, consistent, and compelling message
For what?

The goals of promotion

The communications process

The campaign process

Determine advertising objectives
The specific communication task a campaign should accomplish for a specified target audience during a specified period of time:
Inform?
Persuade?
Or Remind?

Make creative decisions
First creative step is to identify the product benefits
What consumers will receive or achieve by using the product (what is in it for me?)
Not attributes or features, but core benefits, e.g. “Sell the sizzle, not the steak” -anon

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7_lnwNIlJUo Hal Riney – what was the core benefit explained in this ad?

Make creative decisions: campaign appeals
An advertising appeal  identifies a reason for a person to buy a product
Typically play on consumers’ emotions, address some need or want the consumer has or a combination of these
Appeals are developed in an agency by a creative team and then evaluated using market research
Criteria for evaluation may include desirability, exclusiveness or believability
The appeal selected becomes the unique selling proposition

What appeals are used often depends on your understanding of the target market’s behaviours, motivations, characteristics. Read these articles to learn more>>>
https://mumbrella.com.au/what-the-first-yes-ad-for-marriage-equality-did-wrong-469164?fbclid=IwAR25TODLuaUB8cxBSWDo70ZLXo4RxFMzsgVnon0L4sb0xbm4YeTpZbKBn_o
https://www.huffingtonpost.com.au/shirleene-robinson/how-the-yes-campaign-was-successful_a_23286756/

Make media decisions
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WAvp5_bmhhg  Narcos
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mo01RI2-DdU S.Africa
https://youtu.be/9X68dm92HVI?t=297 Ariely
https://www.dw.com/en/controversial-dutch-tv-organ-transplant-show-a-hoax/a-2572346
https://www.commonwealthfund.org/publications/newsletter-article/2018/jun/new-dutch-law-makes-all-adults-organ-donors-default Dutch
See these ads for alternative decisions

Alternative media
Give advertisers innovative ways to reach their target audience and avoid advertising clutter, including:
Video-shopping carts in grocery stores
In-game and in-movie advertising
SMS
“Influencers” >>>
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n2Y3GoN2PGw The Joneses are selling the American Dream

Evaluate the campaign
Can be very difficult to determine the true return on advertising investment
Despite this, marketers spend a considerable amount of time studying advertising effectiveness and its probable impact on sales, market share or awareness
Pre-tests include decisions about appeal choice, layout and media type/vehicle
Post-tests might try to understand what has contributed to the ad’s success or whether it met its original goals

For Assignments
Consider:
Nature of the product
Product Life Cycle
Cost and risk
Type of buying decision
Routine vs complex
Target market characteristics
Degree of knowledge about brand
Geographic dispersion or concentration
